action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 2 achieved MSE 0.4925 with DLinear (shared, no decomposition) + RevIN on CPU for 5 epochs. Training loss (~8.8) and validation loss (~12.5) were still decreasing, indicating underfitting and room for improvement. The 5-epoch run took ~9 min, suggesting per-batch CPU overhead is high with batch_size=32. Analysis: (1) Increasing batch size to 512 will cut DataLoader overhead and better utilize CPU BLAS. (2) Switching to individual linear layers lets each of the 7 features learn its own 96→720 mapping, which should help because features have very different scales (OT ~20-30 vs others ~1-5). (3) Training longer (25 epochs) with ReduceLROnPlateau and weight decay should push validation loss lower without manual tuning. (4) All of this stays well under the parameter budget. Decision: Update train.py with larger default batch size, weight decay, and LR scheduler; run 25 epochs with individual=True and bs=512; verify local predict.py; submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --individual --batch_size 512 --epochs 25 --patience 5 && python predict.py --data_path dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output dummy_out.json && echo LOCAL_PREDICT_OK
